examples.title: Recursive Descent Parser - Build Parse Tree
examples.description: Algorithm to build parse tree using recursive descent parsing
examples.code:
FUNCTION parse_E():
 node = createNode('E')
 node.left = parse_T()
 WHILE current_token in {'+', '-'}:
 op_node = createNode(current_token)
 op_node.left = node
 advance_token()
 op_node.right = parse_T()
 node = op_node
 RETURN node

FUNCTION parse_T():
 node = parse_F()
 WHILE current_token in {'*', '/'}:
 op_node = createNode(current_token)
 op_node.left = node
 advance_token()
 op_node.right = parse_F()
 node = op_node
 RETURN node

FUNCTION parse_F():
 IF current_token is '(':
 advance_token()
 node = parse_E()
 expect(')')
 ELSE:
 node = createNode(current_token)
 advance_token()
 RETURN node
examples.explanation: Recursive descent parser builds parse tree by recursively parsing expressions according to precedence. Each function returns a tree node that becomes part of the larger tree.

examples.title: Parse Tree Traversal Algorithms
examples.description: Preorder, inorder, and postorder traversals for parse trees
examples.code:
// Preorder: Root, Left, Right
FUNCTION preorder(node):
 IF node == NULL: RETURN
 PRINT node.value
 preorder(node.left)
 preorder(node.right)

// Inorder: Left, Root, Right
FUNCTION inorder(node):
 IF node == NULL: RETURN
 inorder(node.left)
 PRINT node.value
 inorder(node.right)

// Postorder: Left, Right, Root
FUNCTION postorder(node):
 IF node == NULL: RETURN
 postorder(node.left)
 postorder(node.right)
 PRINT node.value

// Get yield of parse tree
FUNCTION get_yield(node):
 IF node == NULL: RETURN ""
 IF node.isTerminal:
 RETURN node.value
 RETURN get_yield(node.left) + get_yield(node.right)
examples.explanation: Three traversal methods produce different notations: preorder gives prefix, inorder gives infix, postorder gives postfix notation. Get_yield extracts the terminal string.

examples.title: Bottom-Up Parser - Shift Reduce Construction
examples.description: Building parse tree using shift-reduce parsing (LR parsing)
examples.code:
stack = empty
parse_tree_stack = empty

WHILE not end_of_input:
 token = lookahead
 
 // Shift: push token onto stack
 IF can_shift:
 push(token, stack)
 push(createLeafNode(token), parse_tree_stack)
 advance_input()
 
 // Reduce: combine top elements using production
 WHILE can_reduce(production A→β):
 pop |β| symbols from stack
 pop |β| tree nodes from parse_tree_stack
 
 // Create new internal node
 new_node = createNode(A)
 new_node.children = reverse(popped_nodes)
 push(new_node, parse_tree_stack)
 push(A, stack)

// Final parse tree is at top of parse_tree_stack
RETURN parse_tree_stack.top
examples.explanation: Bottom-up parsers build parse tree by shifting tokens and reducing using grammar productions. Reduces combine child nodes into parent nodes representing non-terminals.
